name: Cu Sith
description: The Cu Sith, also known as the Fairy Dog, is a spectral hound from Scottish folklore, particularly in the Highlands. It is described as a massive, shaggy dog, often the size of a calf, with dark green fur. This creature is said to haunt the moors and glens, its howl a harbinger of death. Legend says its bark can be heard for miles, and those who hear it are warned to reach safety before the third bark, or risk being overcome by terror and possibly death. The Cu Sith is sometimes depicted as a hunter of souls, taking the dying to the afterlife. It is often associated with the fae and considered a servant or guardian of the otherworld. Its appearance is considered an ill omen, and its presence is usually linked to impending doom or tragedy. The Cu Sith is a fearsome creature, deeply rooted in Scottish oral traditions, representing the untamed and often dangerous aspects of the natural world and the power of the supernatural. Stories of the Cu Sith served as warnings to respect the boundaries of the wild and to be wary of the unseen forces that dwell within it. The Cu Sith is often linked to specific clans and territories, further embedding it within the cultural landscape of the Scottish Highlands.
subtitle: The Fairy Dog of the Highlands
appearance: A massive dog, the size of a calf, with shaggy, dark green fur. Its eyes glow with an eerie light, and its paws are disproportionately large. Some tales describe it as having a long, braided tail.
habitat: Scottish Highlands, moors, glens, and forests, often near standing stones or ancient burial grounds.
behavior: A solitary creature, the Cu Sith is said to hunt alone or in small packs. It is fiercely territorial and protective of its domain. Its howl is a warning, and its appearance is an omen of death. It is sometimes believed to hunt humans, particularly women, to take them to the fae realm.

keyMyths.title: The Cu Sith's Howl
keyMyths.description: The Cu Sith's howl is a warning of impending death. Those who hear it must seek shelter before the third bark to avoid its fatal effects.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the Fae
keyMyths.description: The Cu Sith is sometimes seen as a guardian of the fae realm, protecting its secrets and boundaries from human intrusion. It serves the fae queen or other powerful entities.

sources.author: W.Y. Evans Wentz
sources.description: An important collection of folklore from Celtic regions, including accounts of the Cu Sith.
sources.text: An important collection of folklore from Celtic regions, including accounts of the Cu Sith.

sources.author: John Gregorson Campbell
sources.work: Superstitions of the Highlands and Islands of Scotland
sources.description: A detailed account of Scottish superstitions, including beliefs about various mythical creatures like the Cu Sith.
